evals.expected_output: Organize by domain: app/Domains/Order/{Actions,DTOs,Contracts}/; never put Eloquent queries in Controllers; Action classes hold use-case logic; bind Contracts to implementations in AppServiceProvider

evals.prompt: Use DTOs in a Laravel application
evals.expected_output: Create readonly class OrderData { public function __construct(public readonly string $title, public readonly int $amount) {} }; pass between layers instead of raw arrays or Eloquent models

evals.prompt: Set up repository pattern in Laravel
evals.expected_output: Create Contracts/OrderRepository interface; implement EloquentOrderRepository; bind in AppServiceProvider via $this->app->bind(OrderRepository::class, EloquentOrderRepository::class); inject interface

evals.prompt: Organize code by domain in Laravel
evals.expected_output: Group by business domain (User, Order, Payment) not by type (Controllers, Models); each domain dir has Actions/, DTOs/, Contracts/ subdirectories; Eloquent models stay in app/Models/

evals.prompt: Apply clean architecture to a Laravel project
evals.expected_output: Layer flow: Controller → Action → Repository Interface → Eloquent; no Eloquent in Controller; DTOs cross boundaries; bind interfaces to implementations in AppServiceProvider

evals.prompt: Use readonly classes for data transfer in PHP
evals.expected_output: readonly class CreateOrderData { public function __construct(public readonly string $customerId, public readonly int $amount) {} }; immutable by default in PHP 8.1+; no raw arrays between layers

should_not_trigger: Repository pattern in Spring Boot with JPA
Use DTOs in a Go service
